jobs.title: Product Manager
jobs.description:
About the Role: 

Orfium is seeking a talented and passionate Product Manager to join our dynamic team. As a Product Manager, you will be responsible for a specific product or feature set, driving its development from conception through to launch and beyond. You will have a direct impact on our products and will be instrumental in delivering value to our customers. The ideal candidate will have a solid understanding of product management methodologies and a proven ability to work with cross-functional teams to deliver exceptional results.

Responsibilities 

  • Define and own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for your product area.
  • Conduct in-depth market research and customer analysis to identify opportunities and inform product decisions.
  • Translate product strategy into detailed requirements, user stories, and prototypes.
  • Prioritize features and initiatives based on business value, customer impact, and technical feasibility.
  • Provide product direction for a cross-functional team of engineers, designers, and other stakeholders to deliver high-quality products.
  • Effectively communicate product plans, progress, and outcomes to internal and external stakeholders. 
  • Track and report on key product metrics and OKRs.
  • Analyze product performance and use data-driven insights to iterate and improve upon the product.
  • Mentor junior product managers and contribute to the overall growth of the product team.

jobs.benefitsSection:
💰 Competitive salary package and participation in our Stock Options plan
🏠 Hybrid work model with flexibility to support your lifestyle
🏥 Comprehensive private health and life insurance coverage
🌴 Extra paid time off to recharge and take care of yourself
💻 The latest tech equipment to support your productivity and creativity
🌍 A collaborative, inclusive, and international work environment

About Us 

We are a global technology leader transforming the music and entertainment industry through advanced rights management and data solutions. With 700+ team members across offices in Los Angeles, London, Dublin, Athens, Sofia, Tokyo, and more, we partner with top-tier clients such as Sony Music Publishing, Warner Music Group, BBC, and Universal Music Publishing. Our mission is to help creators, rights holders, and media companies track, manage, and monetize content across platforms like YouTube and TikTok. At Orfium, you’ll join a passionate, international team of developers, designers, scientists, and music lovers, all working together in a flexible, hybrid environment where innovation, openness, and ownership are at the heart of everything we do. We are looking forward to meeting with you!

EEO / Accommodation

At Orfium, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and candidates—regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. If you require any accommodations during the application or interview process, please let us know. We’re here to ensure you have a comfortable and fair experience every step of the way.
jobs.requirementsSection:
• 2-5 years of experience in a product management role.
  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
  • Proven track record of managing all aspects of a successful product throughout its lifecycle.
  • Strong understanding of Agile methodologies and experience working in an Agile environment.
  • Experience with product management tools like Jira and Confluence.
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making.
  • Exceptional communication and leadership skills, with the ability to influence and align cross-functional teams.
  • Solid technical background with an understanding of software development and web technologies.
  • Proficiency in English.

Nice to Have 

  • Experience working in the music technology or rights management industry.
  • Experience with B2B SaaS products.
  • Experience in/understanding of ML/AI tools is a strong plus.
  • Familiarity with data analysis and visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker).
  • An MBA or other advanced degree.

company.title: Orfium
company.description:
A few things about Orfium

Orfium is the global technology leader in solving the entertainment industry’s biggest challenges around digital music and broadcast rights management, cue sheets, data, and reporting.

We’re transforming the entertainment ecosystem with industry-leading software and music reporting solutions so that whenever music is played in the world, Orfium is working behind the scenes to support its customers to track it, deliver the data, and help creators, rights holders, and media companies report and monetize the usage.

Orfium works with some of the largest music and entertainment companies in the world, including Warner Music Group, Sony Music Entertainment, Sony Music Publishing, Warner Chappell Music Publishing, Universal Music Publishing Group, Ingrooves, Red Bull, and many more! Our team of 500+ operates from locations including LA, London, Dublin, Tokyo, and Athens.

We’re music lovers, developers, data scientists and designers - all working together to improve the entertainment industry for everyone. Our people are passionate, dedicated and constantly innovating. We’re committed to creating a fair and transparent working environment where everyone can thrive and be themselves.

We are looking for talented people to join our team who are passionate about making a difference!

jobs.title: Product Manager
jobs.description:
Achievement Network (ANet) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to helping all students achieve strong educational outcomes. We partner with school and system leaders to strengthen teaching and learning through the strategic use of assessments, coaching, and tools that drive meaningful results. Individuals who join ANet become part of a dynamic, mission-driven, and collaborative organization focused on making a lasting impact in schools. We are committed to supporting one another and growing together as a team.

Location:  Flexible within the United States

OPPORTUNITY

The Product Manager will define, build, evangelize, and continuously improve ANet’s product line in service of educational equity, translating organizational strategy into a muti-year product roadmap that balances user impact, technical feasibility, and financial sustainability.

TRAVEL

Up to 2-3 times a year

RESPONSIBILITIES

Lead a thoughtful product development process end-to-end

  • Validate hypotheses for how to increase the impact of ANet products
  • Identify and frame critical product questions, define success metrics, and extract insights from complex quantitative and qualitative data to inform strategic and operational decisions
  • Define user flows and designs 
  • Develop and manage the product roadmap, proactively identifying tradeoffs, sequencing priorities across teams, and recommending resource allocation decisions that maximize organizational impact
  • Communicate a clear and compelling product vision across technical and non-technical audiences, facilitate decision-making discussions that surface tradeoffs and risks, and ensure alignment between day-to- day execution and broader organizational strategy 
  • Identify legacy functionality that no longer delivers value for users
  • Lead cross-functional product launches, aligning engineering, content, operations, sales, and partner-facing teams through structured decision-making processes and proactive stakeholder engagement   

Further develop the Product Management function at ANet

  • Establish scalable product discovery, prioritization and feedback systems that increase clarity, efficiency and impact across teams 
  • Contribute to the design of a product learning trajectory that builds the team's skills and perspectives on core product management competencies
  • Prioritize research to stay abreast of market trends, key issues, and competitors, elevating critical insights across the Evaluate product investments through an ROI and impact lens, making recommendations that balance innovation with responsible stewardship of organizational resources 

REQUIREMENTS & COMPETENCIES

In order to be successful in this role, candidates must demonstrate the following:

  • Expertise in area of focus: 
    • At least 5 years of experience as a product manager with a strong understanding of agile product discovery and development practices
    • Strong understanding of a variety of software development practices, a good intuition for complexity level estimation for different types of software requests, and the ability to quickly and thoroughly reach super-user status with a variety of software solutions
  • Critical Thinking & Data Orientation: 
    • Demonstrated ability to extract sophisticated insights from complex data sets, anticipate downstream implications for multiple stakeholders, and advocate for decisions grounded in evidence 
  • Planning and Prioritization: 
    • Demonstrated ability to frame and oversee complex, cross-functional initiatives, aligning stakeholders around shared goals while proactively mitigating risks and dependencies Breaks down work tasks/projects into process steps and executes them effectively, adjusting course as needed
  • Communication: 
    • Ability to influence and align diverse stakeholders - including senior leaders - by tailoring communication, surfacing tradeoffs, and facilitating clear and timely decision-making 

Ideal candidates may also demonstrate the following preferred qualifications:

  • Expertise in area of focus: 
    • Experience in K-12 education, Ed Tech, or the nonprofit space

COMPENSATION

The salary range for this position is between $99,144- $148,716. New hires will start between the range minimum and midpoint ($99,144 - $123,930) based on qualifications assessed during the hiring process, past experience, and internal equity. In order to uphold our commitment to transparent, equitable, competitive, and sustainable compensation, ANet does not negotiate pay.

BENEFITS 

We offer comprehensive benefits to best support our people. Benefits include: medical, dental and vision insurance where ANet pays a portion of the cost of these benefits for employees and their families/domestic partner; generous paid time-off including 10 paid holidays and paid days off between the Christmas and New Year’s holidays; paid parental leave; educational expenses reimbursements;  flexible spending accounts; professional development; a 401(k) plan with a 4% match; short and long-term disability coverage; and basic life and personal accident insurance. 

Conflict of Interest / Outside Work Disclosure

Because this role includes direct instructional coaching and partnership work, candidates must disclose any current consulting, contracting, or employment with organizations that provide instructional coaching, curriculum implementation support, or similar services to schools or districts. ANet reviews outside work to ensure there is no conflict of interest with core services or partner relationships. Individuals currently contracting with organizations that are in direct competition with ANet may not be eligible for this role.

ANet is committed to maximizing the diversity of our organization. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage individuals of all ethnic and racial backgrounds and gender identities to apply to our positions.

Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.title: Product Manager
jobs.description:
Company Description

To apply for this position, you must be a U.S. Citizen, you must currently live in the U.S., you must pass an FBI background check, and you must be willing to take a drug test.

ForceMetrics™ mission is to transform data for social change. We’re striving to make a positive community impact by helping public safety and government agencies better utilize data to benefit every individual. By bringing data-driven innovation to dated systems and processes, ForceMetrics is empowering responders to make better, more informed decisions in the moment, ultimately working to solve some of the toughest societal problems.  

About the role

HOW YOU WILL HAVE IMPACT

  • Work directly with customers and field users to surface problems worth solving and see them through to delivery.
  • Collaborate across Product, Engineering, Customer Success, and Go-To-Market teams to shape and execute the product roadmap.
  • Translate product strategy into clear user stories and detailed requirements that deliver customer value.
  • Take features from zero to one, including ideation, research, development, and successful launch.
  • Enable go-to-market readiness through collaboration with Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success.

WHAT YOU WILL DO

  • Write user stories and functional specs based on customer and stakeholder feedback.
  • Run discovery sessions, conduct user interviews, and review customer feedback to validate product direction.
  • Partner with product and leadership teams to help define the product roadmap and prioritize impactful initiatives.
  • Collaborate with Engineering on timelines and deliverables.
  • Communicate product vision and updates through wireframes, presentations, and internal documentation.
  • Support the evaluation, integration, and onboarding of new datasets and third-party APIs into the platform.
  • Coordinate launches with internal stakeholders and ensure readiness across GTM functions.
  • Serve as the voice of the customer within cross-functional teams.

jobs.benefitsSection:
WHAT WE CAN OFFER

  • A strong sense of mission for impacting real communities and solving complex societal problems
  • Work alongside other top talent in a rewarding and enjoyable culture
  • Company Equity (seed-state)
  • Medical, Dental, Vision & Life Insurance
  • 401(k)
  • 100% Remote
  • Standard holidays and paid time off
  • Salary range for this position is Base Salary of $105K to $130K+ DOE

About ForceMetrics

ForceMetrics was founded in 2020 to provide data analytics and enrichment applications to public safety agencies throughout the United States, with a focus on better community outcomes through data.

We're venture-backed with a diverse founding team led by Andre McGregor, computer engineer and veteran FBI Special Agent turned entrepreneur, alongside a team of data scientists, software engineers, cybersecurity experts, and business operations leaders—many of whom have been law enforcement officers themselves, and have previously built successful companies.

We have a robust set of investors and an advisory board of active/retired police chiefs, government leaders, and technology innovators.

ForceMetrics™ values diversity, and we are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. ForceMetrics™ will not tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind based on any characteristics.
jobs.requirementsSection:
WHAT YOU MUST HAVE

  • Domain experience: 3 to 5 years in a Product Manager role within a SaaS company, ideally in public safety, law enforcement, govtech, or a data-intensive product environment, or direct experience as a law enforcement professional, crime analyst, or public safety practitioner ready to transition into product.
  • Data fluency: You've worked with data in a meaningful way, whether building data products, working with APIs and integrations, or using data operationally to make decisions in the field.
  • Startup experience: You've worked in a fast-moving, high-growth environment where ambiguity is the norm and execution is the expectation.
  • Small team success: You're energized by working on a focused, cross-functional team. You contribute directly, take ownership, and move things forward alongside your teammates.
  • Hands-on mindset: You roll up your sleeves, take ownership, and move things forward without waiting for perfect conditions.
  • End-to-end expertise: You've conducted research, built requirements, and successfully shipped products.
  • Exceptional communication: You can clearly articulate priorities and requirements to both technical teams and leadership.
  • Problem-solving skills: Comfortable navigating ambiguity, prioritizing ruthlessly, and using creative thinking to move forward.
  • Comfort with iterative development: You've worked in sprint-based environments and understand how to ship incrementally.

company.title: ForceMetrics
company.description:
ForceMetrics™ mission is to transform data for social change. We’re striving to make a positive community impact by helping public safety and government agencies better utilize data to benefit every individual. By bringing data-driven innovation to dated systems and processes, ForceMetrics is empowering responders to make better, more informed decisions in the moment, ultimately working to solve some of the toughest societal problems.


Join our team

Our team is driven by a passion for complex challenges, combined with a desire to make a difference. We are dedicated to our mission and provide our team members with the chance to utilize their skills to achieve significant, impactful results.

Competitive Package
We offer competitive salaries, equity, 401k, and always reward hard work.

Professional Growth
Further your development with professional courses and resources.

Fully Remote
We're a nationwide team, work from your home, or anywhere you like.

Parental Leave
We're here to support you and your family with the time you need.

Flexible PTO
We encourage time off and trust our team to take it as they wish.

Work that Matters
Know you're making an impact, helping to create safer communities.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
At ForceMetrics, we know that diversity, equity, and inclusion are vital to our success. We strive to create a welcoming work environment where all team members feel valued and respected. We recognize the unique perspectives and experiences that each person brings to the table, and are committed to fostering an inclusive culture where everyone has the opportunity to thrive. By valuing diversity and promoting equity, we can not only create a better workplace, but also drive innovation and better serve the communities we’re doing this work for.

jobs.title: Product Manager
jobs.description:
We live in a world that runs on physical industries like energy, logistics, and manufacturing. These sectors are essential to our economy and daily lives, yet they are held back by inefficiencies, fragile legacy systems, and decades-old ways of working. This is not just a business problem; it is a societal one. When grids fail, supply chains stall, or factories falter, entire economies are disrupted.

These companies face unique, complex challenges that typical software was never designed to solve. Cogna was founded to change this. Our mission is to give critical industries their own AI-powered Software Factory: a way to build and deploy bespoke digital tools that solve pressing operational challenges.

Since our launch in May 2023, we’ve seen incredible customer traction and revenue growth. We’re backed by leading VCs, including Notion Capital and Hoxton Ventures, and we’re building a world-class team to take on one of the most important challenges of our time. We’re a team of around 75 today, on a path to Series B and scaling towards 150.

Tackling challenges at this scale takes people who are curious, adaptable, and motivated by impact. If our mission resonates with you, we encourage you to apply, even if your experience doesn’t match every requirement. We are committed to building a diverse team that reflects the industries and societies we serve.

We are looking for a commercially-minded, user-focused Product Manager to own the application surfaces our customers interact with directly. Our product surface needs to scale across hundreds of thousands of solution strategists and consultants, providing them access to our AI-run software factory that allows them to build bespoke applications for their customers. You’ll work closely with partners, customers, design, and engineering to ship intuitive, valuable features  and to deepen how much our enterprise customers build with us.

You’ll sit close to the business. We have strong C-suite relationships at our enterprise customers, and those leaders want their teams to do more with us. Your job is to find the easiest ways for those teams to get more value from Cogna and to double down with us.

This role suits someone who thrives in a 0→1 environment but understands how product fits into the wider business. You’ll be joining as we move past the startup stage into scale-up and onto our Series B path, so commercial awareness matters as much as product craft.

What You’ll Do:

  • Understand user needs: Run deep customer discovery to understand needs and behaviours, and turn that into roadmap and strategy decisions.
  • Deepen engagement: Find easy, high-value ways for customer teams to get more from Cogna, build more with us, and double down.
  • Think commercially: Understand product-market fit and our high-level business strategy, including our Series B path  and use it to prioritise which user flows we go after.
  • Drive iterative development: Ship MVPs that solve core user problems, then evolve them based on feedback and usage to maximise user and business value.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally: Partner closely with engineering and design to define, prioritise, and ship features that feel intuitive.
  • Run delivery hands-on: We don’t have TPMs, so you’ll keep dev teams aligned on agreed priorities and track delivery closely in Linear.
  • Decide with low signal: Make good calls with limited customer data, relying on research and intuition where complete signal isn’t available.

What We’re Looking For:

  • Experience: 4+ years in product management, with a track record across both 0→1 work and scaling in high-growth environments.
  • Scale-up experience: You’ve worked at mid-to-large company scale, not only at startups, you know what a scaling journey (e.g. through Series B/C) actually looks like.
  • Commercial awareness: You think about product-market fit and high-level strategy, and you can connect product priorities to the company’s commercial path.
  • Enterprise mindset: You’ve built for enterprise customers and understand how they think about experimentation and satisfaction.
  • Comfortable with AI tooling: You’re happy using tools like Claude Code and Claude Design to prototype and move quickly.
  • Thrives in ambiguity: You don’t need full clarity before taking action, and you stay effective when signal is low.
  • Experimentation mindset: Highly agile, comfortable trying something, measuring it, and changing course quickly.
  • Well-rounded PM: You bring the full gamut of product skills rather than a narrow focus (we’ve found purely analyst, technical-program-management, or testing backgrounds tend to fit less well).

What We Offer

  • Competitive salary and share option plan
  • 25 days annual leave, plus public holidays
  • Excellent pension scheme, with Cogna paying 7.5%
  • Enhanced parental leave: 12 weeks full salary for all parents (plus up to 4 weeks pre-birth for birthing parents)
  • Private healthcare, including family cover and pre-existing conditions
  • Flexible hybrid working with 2 days per fortnight in our Farringdon office
  • Regular social events and offsites
  • Access to a gym and fitness classes in our office building

company.title: Cogna
company.description:
At Cogna, we're working hard to solve the productivity crisis. Lagging productivity is the biggest barrier to economic growth and a major obstacle to addressing our most important issues, such as climate change, healthcare, and social equality.

But it’s not a single problem that can be tackled with a silver-bullet solution, it’s the accumulation of millions of highly specific, context-driven challenges across every facet of every organisation that have to be addressed on their own terms.

Cogna is on a mission to solve this very complex problem. We’re creating an AI-powered platform to understand these ineﬃciencies within customers’ operating models, to define the requirements, and develop the software itself in a fully automated way. The result is high-quality, precision software at scale.

Since our launch in May 2023, we’ve already seen significant traction and customer revenue that outpaces most companies at our stage. We’re backed by top VCs, including Notion Capital and Hoxton Ventures, and we’re growing our world-class team - ex Google, Microsoft, Palantir - to take us to the next level on our mission.

jobs.title: Product Manager
jobs.description:
As a Product Manager, you will own the OASES roadmap and shape how airlines and maintenance organisations actually run their operations day-to-day. You'll be close to the work — talking to mechanics, planners, and ops controllers, not just executives — and your decisions will affect how aircraft get back in the air.

About OASES: 

OASES is a globally used aviation maintenance and engineering platform serving airlines, CAMOs, MROs, and aviation operators. We're transforming it into a real-time aviation operations platform — one that gives customers live visibility, faster decisions, and less manual coordination, with lower transformation risk than the alternatives.

About Valsoft Corp: 

Established in Canada in 2015, Valsoft has grown to a global portfolio of 120+ companies, acquiring and developing vertical market software companies, enabling each business to deliver the best mission-critical solutions for customers in their respective industries. A key tenet of Valsoft’s philosophy is to invest in well-established businesses and foster an entrepreneurial environment that moulds companies into leaders in their respective industries. Valsoft looks to buy, hold, and create value through long-term partnerships with existing management. 

Key Responsibilities:

Strategy & Roadmap

  • Help define and evolve the OASES product strategy and roadmap.
  • Translate operational challenges into product opportunities.
  • Prioritise initiatives based on customer value, operational impact, and strategic fit.
  • Support the evolution toward real-time operational workflows and operational intelligence.

Customer & Market Engagement

  • Work closely with customers, implementation teams, support teams, and sales.
  • Gather operational feedback and workflow pain points.
  • Use telemetry and customer insight to guide roadmap decisions.
  • Understand competitor positioning and market dynamics.

Workflow Ownership

  • Drive improvements across some combination of Maintenance Control, operational planning, engineering workflows, materials and logistics, mobile execution, and integrations.

Delivery

  • Partner with Engineering, Architecture, UX, Support, and Commercial.
  • Define MVP scope, phased delivery, and release alignment.

Product Analytics & Operational Intelligence

  • Promote telemetry-driven product decisions.
  • Support operational KPI definition and measurement.
  • Help evolve operational dashboards, workflow intelligence, and decision-support capabilities.

jobs.benefitsSection:
Why Join Us:

You will help shape the future of aviation operational software by building products that improve:

  • Operational visibility.
  • Execution efficiency.
  • Maintenance coordination.
  • Operational resilience.
  • Real-time decision-making.
  • Opportunity to work on meaningful operational challenges in a global aviation environment while influencing the direction of a widely used aviation platform.
jobs.requirementsSection:
• Bachelor’s degree in business management, computer science, or related field.
  • Enterprise software product management experience, ideally on workflow platforms or complex B2B SaaS.
  • Strong roadmap and prioritisation instincts.
  • Comfortable moving between technical and operational domains.
  • Pragmatic, analytical, and outcome-focused — you can balance strategy with execution realities.
  • Strong communicator across customers, engineers, and commercial teams.

A real bonus: aviation, MRO, airline operations, CAMO workflows, logistics, or other regulated-industry experience. Operational intelligence or workflow automation backgrounds also stand out.

company.title: Valsoft Corporation

Our focus is to acquire and grow vertical market software businesses that provide mission-critical solutions in their respective niche markets. So far, we have acquired over 100+ businesses, and we have over 3,000 employees across 20+ countries. In 2023, Valsoft was named as one of the Best Workplaces in the Financial Services Industry by Great Place to Work®.
